channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Turkey calls for reconciliation between Syria government and opposition to end conflict
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — The recent rapid advance by opposition fighters in Syria shows that Syrian President Bashar Assad must reconcile with his own people and hold dialogue with the opposition, the Turkish foreign minister said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken heads to final NATO foreign ministers meeting of Biden administration with Ukraine in focus
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is heading back to Europe for what will likely be the last high-level NATO meeting before the Biden administration leaves office next month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel says soldier thought to have been taken hostage is now presumed dead
item.description: The Israeli military said Monday an Israeli American soldier who was believed to have been captured alive by Hamas in its Oct. 7, 2023, attack was killed that day and his body taken into the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Guinea stadium stampede kills 56 people following clashes at soccer match, authorities say
item.description: CONAKRY, Guinea (AP) — At least 56 people, including children, have died after fans at a soccer game in Guinea allegedly clashed over a disputed call by the referee, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli American soldier from Long Island killed: Military
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) -- The Israeli military said Monday an Israeli American soldier who was believed to have been captured alive by Hamas in its Oct. 7, 2023, attack was killed that day and his body taken into the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Stars, heads of state, solemn rituals and high-security celebrations for Notre Dame's reopening
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The reopening of Notre Dame this coming weekend is going to be a high-security affair, with a repeat of some measures used during the Paris Olympics and the sealing-off to tourists of the cathedral's island location in the heart of the French capital.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC

item.title: German leader Olaf Scholz vows more Ukraine aid, defends his phone call with Putin
item.description: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz has visited Ukraine for the first time in more than two years and vowed to keep supporting Kyiv in the war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Over 40 people hospitalized in Georgia during protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — A third night of protests in the Georgian capital against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union left 44 people hospitalized, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria launches counterattacks in an attempt to halt insurgency, as Iran's top diplomat meets Assad
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian military rushed reinforcements to the northwest and launched airstrikes Sunday in an attempt to push back insurgents who seized the country’s largest city of Aleppo, as Iran pledged to help the government counter the surprise offensive.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan's president visits East-West think tank as China criticizes his 2-day visit to Hawaii
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Taiwan's president visited a U.S. State Department-funded think tank and educational institution Sunday on the second day of a two-day visit to Hawaii that's part of a Pacific island tour that has already triggered criticism from Beijing.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladesh court acquits Zia’s son and 48 others convicted in 2004 deadly grenade attack
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh’s High Court on Sunday acquitted former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia’s son, Tarique Rahman, and 48 others, overturning their verdicts in a deadly 2004 grenade attack on a political rally.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A landmark climate change case will open at the top UN court as island nations fear rising seas
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The top United Nations court will take up the largest case in its history on Monday, when it opens two weeks of hearings into what countries worldwide are legally required to do to combat climate change and help vulnerable nations fight its devastating impact.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania’s leftists set to top polls in parliamentary election, but far-right populists see big gains
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania’s Social Democratic Party looked set to win the most votes in Sunday’s parliamentary election, incomplete data showed, while far-right populists were on track to make significant gains in the country’s legislature.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ireland's incumbent parties look likely to hang on to power after a fractured election
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — Ireland’s two long-dominant center-right parties looked likely to form a new government as results came in Sunday from a fractured national election, though with a reduced vote share and complex coalition negotiations ahead.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Photos: New Starbucks gives customers view of a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Customers have to pass a military checkpoint before entering the observatory where the new Starbucks has opened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Largest gold deposit in the world worth $83 billion found in China
item.description: A discovery of gold reserves, estimated to be worth $83 billion, was made in China last month. China is the world's largest gold producer, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A new Belgium law gives sex workers contracts, benefits and safety protection
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Starting Sunday, sex workers in Belgium will be able to sign formal employment contracts and gain labor rights on par with those in other professions in a legal breakthrough some call a “revolution.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ireland election: Incumbent center-right parties likely to retain power, but left-wing Sinn Fein holds water
item.description: Ireland's election results have yet to be finalized, but exit polling suggests a coalition government between the center-right Fine Gael and Fianna Fáil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plastic treaty talks draw to a close with production limits still under debate
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiations on a treaty to end plastic pollution are drawing to a close Sunday as nations continue debating whether part of that should be putting limits on plastic production.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iceland votes for a new parliament after political disagreements force an early election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Icelanders voted to elect a new parliament Saturday after disagreements over immigration, energy policy and the economy forced Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson to pull the plug on his coalition government and call an early election.
item.dc:creator: MARCO DI MARCO and DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Brazilian police say Bolsonaro plotted a coup to stay in office
item.description: Brazil's Federal Police in November formally accused former President Jair Bolsonaro and more than 30 other people of attempting a coup to keep him in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump lauds 'productive' meeting with Trudeau amid tension over tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump confirmed his Friday meeting with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling the conversation "productive.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Former Israeli diplomat: Israel-Hamas ceasefire not likely before Biden leaves
item.description: Former Israeli diplomat Alon Pinkas predicted Saturday that a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Palestinian militant group Hamas in Gaza is unlikely before President Biden departs the White House early next year.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Zelensky suggests NATO membership could halt 'hot phase’ of war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky suggested this week that NATO welcoming Ukraine into the alliance could halt the “hot stage” of the war, while Russia would temporarily keep the territory it overtook.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: What to know about sudden rebel gains in Syria's 13-year war and why it matters
item.description: The 13-year civil war in Syria has roared back into prominence with rebels' surprise capture of Aleppo, one of the country’s largest cities and an ancient hub of Middle East culture and commerce.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 children and a woman crushed to death outside Gaza bakery amid food shortage
item.description: Two children and a woman were crushed to death Friday as a crowd of Palestinians pushed to get bread at a bakery in the Gaza Strip amid a worsening food crisis in the war-ravaged territory, medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: School bus driver charged with holding girl captive for 12 years
item.description: A school bus driver in Colombia allegedly held a girl captive for more than a decade until she recently escaped, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel cease-fire deal has critics worried over Biden, Obama officials looking to stymie incoming Trump admin
item.description: After 14 months of war between Israel and Hezbollah, a cease-fire went into effect this week amid allegations that the truce happened due to pressure put on Israel despite denials from the administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll rises to at least 15 after landslides bury 40 homes in eastern Uganda
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — At least 15 people have died and 113 others are missing after landslides buried homes in six villages in eastern Uganda, police said Thursday. Another 15 injured people have been rescued and admitted to Buluganya Health Center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation
